query: Save Wimbledon Park Court of Appeal challenge status date hearing 2026

{"query":"Save Wimbledon Park Court of Appeal challenge status date hearing 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1788,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.russell-cooke.co.uk/news-and-insights/news/save-wimbledon-park-granted-permission-to-take-judicial-review-to-court-of-appeal","title":"Save Wimbledon Park granted permission to take judicial review to Court of Appeal","content":"On 18 November 2025 Save Wimbledon Park (SWP) was granted permission to take its judicial review of planning approval to the Court of Appeal.
The Court of Appeal has now granted permission to appeal Mr Justice Saini’s decision.
Lord Justice Holgate granted SWP permission on Wednesday this week with confirmation that it will continue to benefit from Aarhus costs protection.
The Court of Appeal proceedings will be heard in 2026 and are expected to run over the course of two days.
If you would like to speak with a member of the team you can contact our regulation and public law solicitors by telephone on +44 (0)20 3826 7524 or complete our enquiry form.
A campaign group fighting against ‘unlawful’ plans to expand the Wimbledon tennis grounds will take its case to the High Court next week...
Since qualifying over forty years ago, John Gould has made a significant contribution to the sphere of legal services regulation and legal knowledge...
Russell-Cooke’s Senior Partner John Gould and regulatory partner Michael Stacey, with contributions and support from Jack Rogers..."},{"url":"https://www.standard.co.uk/news/london/wimbledon-expansion-ruling-court-of-appeal-b1258890.html","title":"Wimbledon expansion opponents granted permission to fight go-ahead ruling in Court of Appeal | The Standard","content":"In July, Mr Justice Saini dismissed Save Wimbledon Park's (SWP) High Court legal action against the Greater London Authority (GLA) over its 2024 decision to grant planning permission for the expansion.
In a court order dated November 13, Lord Justice Holgate said: "The grounds of appeal are arguable with a real prospect of success.
A date for the appeal hearing, which is set to be held over two days, is yet to be confirmed.
A GLA spokesperson said: "The Mayor believes this scheme will bring a significant range of benefits including environmental, economic, social and cultural benefits to the local area, the wider capital and the UK economy."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwyr7e394p0o","title":"Plans to expand Wimbledon can go ahead, judge rules","content":"Following the ruling on Monday, SWP said it has been "advised that it should" seek to challenge the decision, and that it believed the GLA "did make a significant legal error in the way it dealt with the special legal status of the park".
Sir Sadiq Khan recused himself from the process after previously expressing public support for the development and planning permission for the scheme was granted by Jules Pipe, London's deputy mayor for planning.
He said this meant any plans could not "restrict its use so as not to impair the appreciation of the general public of the extent or openness of the golf course land".
A hearing in that case is due to take place in January 2026.
Sir Sadiq said the ruling was "welcome news" that will "cement Wimbledon's reputation as the greatest tennis competition in the world"."},{"url":"https://www.itv.com/news/london/2025-07-08/legal-challenge-over-wimbledon-expansion-heard-at-high-court","title":"Protesters gather as court rules land for Wimbledon expansion can not have greater legal protection | ITV News London","content":"Protesters have gathered outside the Royal Courts of Justice where a campaign group’s legal challenge against plans to almost triple the size of the Wimbledon tennis site is being heard at the High Court.
After Merton Council approved the plans, but Wandsworth Council rejected them, the Mayor of London’s office took charge of the application.
Dozens of campaigners attended Tuesday’s hearing before Mr Justice Saini, which is due to conclude on Wednesday."}]}
